paper_title: Paleoenvironmental Studies on Marine Sediments of the Chilean Continental Margin

methods.description: This method involves collecting marine sediments from the Chilean Continental margin to study terrigenous and biogenic materials. The goal is to reconstruct environmental conditions using fossil remains and analyze sedimentation patterns.

steps.description: Collect sediment samples using sediment traps and from the sediment surface of the ocean floor.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples using sediment traps and surface collection

steps.description: Use X-Ray Fluorescence scanning to infer paleoenvironmental conditions from sediment cores.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze sediment cores using X-Ray Fluorescence scanning

steps.description: Isolate the terrigenous fraction by removing the organic sediment fraction using peroxide, acids, and bases.
steps.searchable_summary: isolate terrigenous fraction by removing organic components

steps.description: Analyze the grain-size of the terrigenous fraction using a Micromeritics Sedigraph and a Beckmann Coulter laser particle sizer.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze grain-size using Sedigraph and laser particle sizer

steps.description: Analyze the clay-mineralogical composition of the terrigenous fraction using X-ray diffraction (XRD).

methods.description: This method focuses on separating and analyzing the biogenic fraction of sediments to study fossil remains and determine main biogenic sediment constituents.

steps.description: Wet sieve sediments into representative size classes for the respective fossil remains of interest, dry them, and study using a microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve and dry sediments, then study under microscope

steps.description: Determine main biogenic sediment constituents such as organic carbon, carbonate, and biogenic opal following standard procedures.
steps.searchable_summary: determine biogenic constituents using standard procedures

steps.description: Reconstruct paleo sea surface temperatures (SSTs) based on the alkenone method.
